title: VERITAS a Believer in Itanium
author: Clint Boulton
abstract: VERITAS extends Foundation Suite 2.2 and Cluster Server 2.2 to Intel Itanium 2 on Red Hat Linux 3.0. IDC forecasts the Itanium-Linux server market reaching $2B by 2008. Aberdeen Group analyst Peter Kastner notes 400 applications have been certified on Itanium architecture — addressing the historical Itanium application-ecosystem complaint.
importance_rationale: Captures a peak-optimism moment for Itanium's enterprise Linux trajectory — months before AMD64 / Opteron's surge proved decisive in the 64-bit x86 transition.
relevance_rationale: Itanium was effectively defeated by AMD64 by 2006-2008; last shipment 2021. The $2B IDC forecast did not materialize.
prescience_rationale: Both IDC's $2B-by-2008 forecast and the article's optimistic Itanium framing aged poorly. Kastner's 400-applications certification figure was factual but did not save Itanium from architectural defeat by AMD64.
